prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of formaldehyde was computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level of theory in the gas phase (charge 0, multiplicity 1, neutral). The PySCF backend computed the neutral, cation (N−1, doublet), and anion (N+1, doublet) states using Mulliken partial charges. The most electrophilic site is the carbon atom with f⁺ (electrophilic Fukui index) = 0.70839 and a dual descriptor of 0.59595, indicating strong activation toward nucleophilic attack. The most nucleophilic site is the oxygen atom with f⁻ (nucleophilic Fukui index) = 0.46742. All states converged successfully; charge conservation checks (sum of Fukui indices ≈ 1.0) passed. A visualization (bar chart of f⁺, f⁻, dual descriptor per atom) was generated. The calculation is trustworthy. Method: B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF), gas phase, neutral singlet parent with doublet ionic states.</prose>
<parameter name="n_atoms">4
